what_worked_well.observation: Test fixture blocking by pre-tool-use hook detected dangerous content patterns accurately
what_worked_well.impact: Security validation working correctly - prevented potential test fixture with destructive command samples from being created

what_worked_well.observation: Phase state validation CLI gracefully handled missing installer module via backward compatibility
what_worked_well.impact: Workflow continued despite missing import, allowing Phase 01-02 completion

what_worked_well.observation: Test suite successfully validated grep pattern specificity across 30 tests
what_worked_well.impact: All 30 tests passing, confirming correct pattern matching prevents false positives

areas_for_improvement.issue: Phase state validation CLI requires sys.path manipulation to import from installer module
areas_for_improvement.root_cause: installer module not on default Python path when CLI imported directly; phase_commands.py tries to import PhaseState from installer.phase_state

areas_for_improvement.issue: Pre-tool-use hook blocks test fixture creation when sample logs contain blocked patterns like 'rm -rf'
areas_for_improvement.evidence: .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use.sh:114 (BLOCKED_PATTERNS includes 'rm -rf') and test-ac2-auto-approve-count.sh requires commented fixture data
areas_for_improvement.root_cause: Hook pattern matching is content-aware but doesn't distinguish between sample/fixture data (intended to be harmless) and actual dangerous commands being executed

areas_for_improvement.issue: Grep pattern matching test documentation unclear about 'Decision:' prefix requirement
areas_for_improvement.evidence: devforgeai/specs/Stories/STORY-200:43 specifies 'grep AUTO-APPROVE' but implementation requires 'Decision: AUTO-APPROVE'
areas_for_improvement.root_cause: Story acceptance criteria SVC-002 documents pattern as 'AUTO-APPROVE' without the full 'Decision:' prefix, causing ambiguity during implementation

recommendations.title: Fix phase state validation CLI installer module import path resolution
recommendations.description: Move sys.path manipulation to module initialization level to prevent import errors when phase_commands.py is imported via different import paths.
recommendations.implementation_code: In .claude/scripts/devforgeai_cli/__init__.py, add proper path resolution for installer module. Remove sys.path manipulation from phase_commands.py:27-32.

recommendations.title: Document safe vs dangerous grep patterns for hook telemetry testing
recommendations.description: Update story specification to clarify that auto-approval grep pattern must match full decision marker 'Decision: AUTO-APPROVE' not just 'AUTO-APPROVE' substring.
recommendations.implementation_code: Update SVC-002 description from 'grep AUTO-APPROVE' to 'grep "Decision: AUTO-APPROVE"'.

recommendations.title: Add fixture data safeguard to pre-tool-use hook for test environments
recommendations.description: Enhance pre-tool-use.sh to allow fixture creation in test directories by adding an exception for paths containing '/fixtures/' or '/tests/'.
recommendations.implementation_code: Add check: if command or file path contains '/fixtures/' or '/tests/', treat blocked patterns as sample data and log warning instead of blocking.

recommendations.title: Add shell script test naming convention documentation to test-automator
recommendations.description: Test automator agent currently has no documented pattern for naming shell script test files. Add guidance specifying naming convention for Bash test files.
recommendations.implementation_code: Add section '## Shell Script Test Naming Convention' with pattern: test-ac{n}-{description}.sh for acceptance criteria tests.

patterns_observed: Import path resolution complexity when modules span multiple directories (installer vs devforgeai_cli)
Security hooks correctly blocking dangerous patterns even in non-execution contexts (log fixtures)
Specification ambiguity when grep patterns documented without full marker context
anti_patterns_detected: Sys.path manipulation in function scope (phase_commands.py:27-32) instead of module initialization - violates single responsibility principle
constraint_analysis: Context file tech-stack.md constraint (lines 206-210) for native tools over Bash was respected throughout - all 30 tests use native Bash testing framework rather than external tools. Pre-tool-use hook constraints effective in catching both legitimate security violations and fixture data false positives, suggesting hook pattern definitions need refinement for test environment distinction.
